content.content:
- content: 'Marketplace case labels describe process stages rather than a universal

    recommendation. A seller-resolution status means the seller-side handling

    stage is active or recorded. A step-in-request status records whether a

    marketplace intervention request has been submitted or accepted. A

    platform-review status means the marketplace is reviewing the existing

    case. The current order record must be consulted to determine which label

    applies; this glossary does not identify the responsible party for any

    particular transaction.

    '
  metadata: {}
  rank: 1
  title: Marketplace Case Status Glossary
  url: null

args.query: site:ebay.com/help buyer protection seller refund 2 business days return delivered eBay Money Back Guarantee

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Ask eBay to step in and help for buyers

  - url: https://www.ebay.com/help/buying/returns-refunds/ask-ebay-step-help-buyers?id=4701

  - source: eBay

  - domain: ebay.com


  ## Content


  A buyer can ask eBay to step in when there is a problem with an order and the seller
  has not been able to resolve it.


  The step-in option is not always available immediately. For many return or non-delivery
  requests, more than three business days must have passed since the request was opened.
  If the option is not shown in the order details, the request may not yet be eligible.


  A buyer does not have to escalate as soon as eligibility begins. When the buyer
  and seller are still actively working toward a solution, the buyer may allow additional
  time. Requests can close after prolonged inactivity, so the case should not be left
  unattended indefinitely.


  A buyer may also seek eBay''s help when return tracking shows delivery to the seller
  more than two business days earlier but no refund has been received.


  The buyer requests intervention from the relevant purchase or return details and
  confirms a reason. After eBay steps in, eBay reviews the case information and communicates
  the outcome to both sides. If the outcome includes a refund, it is generally returned
  to the original payment method.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Ask eBay to step in and help for buyers
url: https://www.ebay.com/help/buying/returns-refunds/ask-ebay-step-help-buyers?id=4701

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: eBay Money Back Guarantee policy

  - url: https://www.ebay.com/help/policies/ebay-money-back-guarantee-policy/ebay-money-back-guarantee-policy?id=4210

  - source: eBay

  - domain: ebay.com


  ## Content


  The eBay Money Back Guarantee describes how eligible order problems are handled
  when an item does not arrive, is faulty or damaged, or does not match the listing.


  For an item that does not match its listing, the buyer begins by opening a return
  request. The seller is expected to respond and provide a solution. When a return
  is accepted, the buyer must send the item by the date shown in the return details.


  After tracking confirms that the returned item reached the seller, the seller normally
  has a limited period to issue the refund. The applicable refund deadline is shown
  in the case details; some cases may allow additional time.


  If the seller does not respond, does not issue the refund by the stated deadline,
  or the buyer and seller cannot resolve the problem, either party may ask eBay to
  step in. eBay may also intervene automatically in some situations.


  When eBay reviews an item-not-as-described case, it may consider whether both parties
  met their return obligations, whether the listing complied with eBay rules, and
  whether return delivery can be proved. If the seller did not meet the required return
  obligations, eBay may refund the buyer and recover the amount from the seller.

  '
metadata: {}
title: eBay Money Back Guarantee policy
url: https://www.ebay.com/help/policies/ebay-money-back-guarantee-policy/ebay-money-back-guarantee-policy?id=4210

For order **EBY-260618-1047**, the issue is being handled by **eBay now—not the seller or Apple**.

What I found:

- The AirPods were classified as **item not as described**. The device serial number (**H2YQ91K0X2**) does not match the box serial number (**J7M4P6LQ9T**), and the device was activated before the order.
- The seller, **TechRenew Outlet**, accepted the return and issued an eBay return label.
- USPS shows the return was delivered to the seller on **July 2, 2026**.
- As of the recorded check on **July 8**, the seller had issued **no refund or replacement**.
- Your request for marketplace intervention was accepted on **July 7**.
- The case is currently **open and under eBay platform review**: **CASE-EBY-7319042**.

Under eBay’s published procedure, buyers can ask eBay to intervene when tracking shows delivery to the seller and no refund has been received after the applicable period. Since eBay review has already started, **eBay’s case team is the responsible party for the next decision**. The seller may still be contacted by eBay, but you should not need to send the item again or pursue Apple warranty service for this marketplace dispute.

final_answer:
For order **EBY-260618-1047**, the issue is being handled by **eBay now—not the seller or Apple**.

What I found:

- The AirPods were classified as **item not as described**. The device serial number (**H2YQ91K0X2**) does not match the box serial number (**J7M4P6LQ9T**), and the device was activated before the order.
- The seller, **TechRenew Outlet**, accepted the return and issued an eBay return label.
- USPS shows the return was delivered to the seller on **July 2, 2026**.
- As of the recorded check on **July 8**, the seller had issued **no refund or replacement**.
- Your request for marketplace intervention was accepted on **July 7**.
- The case is currently **open and under eBay platform review**: **CASE-EBY-7319042**.

Under eBay’s published procedure, buyers can ask eBay to intervene when tracking shows delivery to the seller and no refund has been received after the applicable period. Since eBay review has already started, **eBay’s case team is the responsible party for the next decision**. The seller may still be contacted by eBay, but you should not need to send the item again or pursue Apple warranty service for this marketplace dispute.
